free_text: Two Ge(Li)-detectors having an effective volume
of 42 and 27 cm3. The corresponding resolution was
2.15 and 2.85 keV for the 1.332 MeV peak of 60Co.
With the natural silver foils the sum of the excitation
functions of the reactions of both silver isotopes
leading to the same reaction products was determined.
Subtracting from this sum the contribution of the
reactions with Ag-107, determined by irradiations of
enriched Ag-107 foils, the excitation functions of the
corresponding reactions on Ag-109 were obtained.
* the analysis of the gamma-ray spectra.
The uncertainty due to the analysis of the gamma-
spectra differs from case to case. Much greater
uncertainties were taken into account in those cases
where complex peak evaluation and subtraction of
activities could not be avoided during the calculation
of the cross sections. In many of these cases only a
rough lower limit estimation of the error could be
The uncertainty due to foil thickness;
The uncertainty due to beam current;
The uncertainty due to detector efficiency;
